name: Prosiutto and Mushroom Ravioli with Fried Sage
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese; grated plus 1/4 cup for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. When the oil is hot, saute the shallots for 30 seconds.
3. Add the sliced wild mushrooms and saute for 2 minutes.
4. Add the minced garlic and julienned prosciutto to the pan. Cook for an additional minute.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Remove from heat and transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
7. Stir in the grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese and let it cool.
8. Slice the sheets of pasta into thirds.
9. Using a hand ravioli mold, place one sheet of pasta down. Spoon 2 tablespoons of the mushroom mixture into each square.
10. Place another sheet of pasta over the filling.
11. Press the two sheets together using a wooden bowl, ensuring the edges are slightly moistened to help seal the ravioli.
12. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the ravioli for 3-4 minutes or until they float to the surface for about 1 minute.
13. Remove the pasta with a slotted spoon and drain well.
14. Turn the cooked ravioli into a bowl containing truffle oil, reserving some oil for frying sage leaves.
15. Season with salt and pepper.
16. Heat oil in a small pan over medium heat. Add the sage leaves and fry for 30 seconds, or until crispy.
17. Remove the sage leaves with a slotted spoon and drain on a paper-lined plate.
18. Season with salt and pepper.
19. Place the cooked ravioli on a serving platter. Drizzle with truffle oil.
20. Garnish with the remaining Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese and crispy fried sage leaves.
